operating_state: Total revenue increased 3% to $28.6 billion driven by Family of Apps advertising growth, while Reality Labs revenue declined 51% from lower Meta Quest sales. Ad impressions increased 26% offset by 17% price decline reflecting iOS changes and regulatory targeting constraints. Revenue growth concentrated in online commerce, healthcare, and entertainment verticals despite macroeconomic pressures.
cost_structure: Total costs increased 10% to $21.4 billion including restructuring charges, with research and development expenses surging 22% supporting AI initiatives and Reality Labs investments. Family of Apps operating margin compressed to 40% while Reality Labs operating losses widened significantly. Management lowered full-year expense outlook to $86-92 billion reflecting restructuring benefits.
material_events: Executed $9.22 billion in share repurchases with board authorizing additional $40 billion program, bringing total remaining authorization to $41.73 billion. Announced March 2023 layoffs affecting approximately 10,000 employees with total restructuring charges expected at $3-5 billion for 2023. Chief Accounting Officer Susan Taylor and Chief Business Officer Marne Levine resigned during the quarter.
macro_exposures: Challenging macroeconomic environment including inflation and rising interest rates continued pressuring advertising demand and marketer spending commitments. Irish Data Protection Commission expected to issue final decision requiring EU data transfer suspension with compliance measures by Q4 2023. Foreign exchange headwinds reduced revenue by approximately $816 million year-over-year.
forward_outlook: Management expects total restructuring charges of $3-5 billion for full-year 2023 with substantial completion by year-end and remaining layoff notifications scheduled for May 2023. Capital expenditures anticipated at $30-33 billion focused on data center infrastructure and Reality Labs hardware development. Reality Labs operating losses expected to increase as metaverse investments continue alongside sustained AI infrastructure investment.
uncertainty_profile: Legal-related accruals increased to $5.5 billion reflecting ongoing regulatory and litigation matters including FTC complaints seeking Instagram and WhatsApp divestiture. Ongoing IRS transfer pricing disputes could result in up to $9.0 billion additional federal tax liability with Tax Court opinion expected in 2024. European privacy regulators conducting investigations with significant accrued loss contingencies.
